reforms.description: The New Zealand government is enhancing its innovation instruments by promoting co-funding for R&D activities and improving links between public research organisations and firms. This includes tying public R&D funding to private-sector funding and enhancing public-private interaction through personnel exchanges.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between public and private sectors is expected to boost R&D productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms significantly improve the innovation support system but do not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The incoming government has taken some steps to reverse this trend. First, it established a new ministerial portfolio of regulatory reform.

reforms.description: New Zealand aims to improve its business environment for foreign direct investment (FDI) by targeting best practices and reducing FDI restrictions. Additionally, the Limited Partnership Act 2008 was passed to facilitate the development of the venture capital market, allowing limited-liability partnerships common in venture capital.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the business environment and facilitating venture capital is expected to enhance startup growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Limited Partnership Act 2008 has been passed and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: These reforms significantly enhance the startup ecosystem but do not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: New Zealand should target best practices by emulating leading countries, such as Belgium, which show that it is possible to reduce FDI restrictions to well below OECD averages.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government plans to disestablish the Fast Forward fund, which financed R&D investments in the pastoral and food industries, and replace it with direct annual funding for primary-sector R&D while retaining aspects of industry partnership and co-funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Direct funding for R&D is expected to enhance investment in primary-sector innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D funding is allocated in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: The new government has indicated that it will disestablish the fund and replace it with direct annual funding for primary-sector R&D.

reforms.description: New Zealand is enhancing its policies to strengthen international research mobility, which includes providing scholarships and fellowships to attract researchers into the country and facilitating opportunities for studying abroad.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled researchers is expected to enhance the overall research capacity and innovation in New Zealand.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances human capital policies but does not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Policies that strengthen international research mobility can keep scientists up to date with the latest developments in their field.

reforms.description: The New Zealand Health Strategy aims to improve population health by reallocating resources based on health needs and promoting collaborative health care delivery models, focusing on patient wellness and chronic disease management.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reallocating resources to health needs is expected to enhance health outcomes and innovation in health care.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially implemented since 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in health sector R&D priorities.
reforms.source_quote: The New Zealand Health Strategy, introduced in 2001 by the previous government, set out its ambitions for improving population health by means of increased equity in the allocation of available resources according to health need.

reforms.description: The new government has reaffirmed the principles of the New Zealand Health Strategy, emphasizing the need for innovation in clinical care while also focusing on hospital efficiency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reaffirming the focus on innovation in clinical care is expected to enhance health service delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform reaffirms existing strategies rather than introducing new policies.
reforms.source_quote: The new government has reaffirmed the principles of the New Zealand Health Strategy, particularly regarding the need for innovation in clinical care.
